verdict_reasoning: BAX is canonically cytosolic/mitochondrial-outer-membrane and drives MOMP during apoptosis. However, during apoptosis and immunogenic cell death, BAX (along with BAK) translocates and is exposed on the outer mitochondrial membrane and has documented cell-death-associated externalization on dying cells. This is a cell-state-induced surface story, not a baseline anchor, so contextual rather than yes. Not pMHC-only.
